summary: Comprehensive frontend testing of Stripe-style mega menu navigation completed. All 14 requested features passed. Desktop hover+click behavior, escape key, click outside, mouse leave delays, mobile hamburger menu with accordion - all working correctly. All 18 new placeholder pages render properly.

passed: Desktop mega menu opens on hover for Solutions (4-column panel with Payments & Processing, Customer Engagement, Financial Services, Business Operations)
Desktop mega menu opens on hover for Industries (4-column panel with Food & Hospitality, Retail Businesses, Health & Wellness, Professional Services)
Desktop mega menu opens on hover for POS & Hardware (4-column panel with POS Systems, Payment Terminals, Self-Ordering & Kiosk Systems, Online Payments)
Desktop mega menu opens on hover for Resources (4-column panel with Education, Research, Content, Documents)
Desktop standard dropdown opens on hover for Pricing (simple list with 6 items)
Desktop standard dropdown opens on hover for Partnerships (simple list with 4 items)
Desktop standard dropdown opens on hover for Company (simple list with 5 items)
Only one menu open at a time - switching from Solutions to Industries closes Solutions automatically
Click toggles menu open/closed on desktop - tested with Company dropdown
Mouse leave closes menu after ~200ms delay
Escape key closes open menu (tested with Solutions panel)
Click outside closes all menus
Mobile hamburger button visible and opens full-screen menu
Mobile accordion expands sections on tap with grouped category headings
Mobile accordion - only one section open at a time (tested Solutions→Industries)
Mobile tapping a link closes the mobile menu and navigates

All mega menu links navigate to correct pages (tested sample from each section: Solutions→Payment Processing, Industries→Restaurants, POS→Smart Terminals, Pricing→POS Subscription, Partnerships→White Label, Company→Service Areas)
Placeholder pages render correctly: /solutions/merchant-rewards, /pos/overview, /pricing, /company/support

Additional placeholder pages working: /solutions/lines-of-credit, /pos/pax-terminals, /pos/clover-kiosk, /pos/qr-ordering, /pos/virtual-terminal, /pos/payment-links, /pricing/free-pos, /solutions/ach-processing, /solutions/atm-deployer
Header CTAs (Book Demo, Get Free POS System) always visible on desktop
Mobile CTAs (Book Demo, Get Free POS System) visible at bottom of mobile menu
Navigation is sticky at top of page (verified y position = 0 after scrolling)

critical_code_review_comments: Navigation component well-structured with proper separation of concerns (MegaPanel, DropdownPanel, MobileAccordion sub-components)
Hover delays properly implemented: 120ms open delay, 200ms close delay using useRef timers
Proper cleanup of timers in clearTimers callback using useCallback
Escape key listener properly set up with focus return to trigger button
Click outside detection working with navRef and mousedown listener
All interactive elements have proper data-testid attributes for testing
Mobile accordion correctly groups items by heading from mega menu columns
Route change effect properly closes all menus (useEffect on location.pathname)
Good accessibility: aria-expanded, aria-haspopup, aria-controls attributes present
Minor: ChevronDown rotation animation smooth with transition-transform duration-200
seed_data_creation: N/A - frontend navigation with static menu data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Stripe-style mega menu navigation fully tested and working. Desktop: hover with delays (120ms open, 200ms close), click toggle, escape key, click outside - all working. Mobile: hamburger button opens full-screen overlay, accordion behavior with one section at a time, tap link closes menu - all working. Menu types: Solutions/Industries/POS & Hardware/Resources are 'mega' type (4-column panels), Pricing/Partnerships/Company are 'dropdown' type (simple lists). All 18 new placeholder pages render correctly with PlaceholderPage component pattern showing title, Coming Soon, and CTA buttons.
